question:
As a teenager, the sight of Mount Kilimanjaro rising majestically above the Serengeti made Vern Jones desperately want to climb it. Finally in 2014, at the age of 62, Vern finally realised his dream.

Vern Jones was accompanied by ________ when he climbed Kilimanjaro in 2014.

Lisa Ludwinski started her business from her parents' kitchen. What followed was an industrious few years — she took a business class, took on an intern, and raised money for her own bakery.

Lisa raised money for her own bakery in ________, Michigan.

In 2015, Tracy Warshal was in a queue at a busy shop when the man in front of her realised he'd forgotten his wallet. Tracy paid for the stranger's milk and other items.

Tracy Warshal was in a queue at a busy shop during ________.

Last year, I joined a group that wanted to clean up one of the beaches near ________, where I live. It is very popular with tourists who often leave litter there. I used to go to the beach every weekend and pick up the litter, all for free!

Near which Jordanian city did this person join a beach clean-up group?
The documentary 'Unconquered: Helen Keller in Her Story' was first made in ________.
The documentary 'A Beautiful Planet' was filmed by ________ from the International Space Station.
In the article 'Sacked for Being Too Old!', Professor Paul Ewart worked at ________ University.
The quote at the beginning of Unit 7 is: 'Our lives begin to end the day we become silent about things that ________.'
In the article about Harar, how long has nobody been attacked by a hyena in the city?
The charity in the radio report about water is doing three of the following. Which one is it NOT doing?
Husam, the charity worker, plans to do the same work at ________ next week.
Husam plans to help another village next week because he thinks it's really important to help ________.
choices: all the villages in the area

question: The verb 'admit' follows the pattern: verb + ________.
The verb 'encourage' follows the pattern: verb + ________.
The verb 'apologise for' follows the pattern: verb + ________.
The verb 'accuse somebody of' follows the pattern: verb + ________.
What does the phrase 'It goes without saying that...' express?
Which suffix is used to form the verb 'clarify'?
Which suffix is used to form the adjective 'productive'?
Which suffix is used to form the adjective 'formal'?

'I'm really sorry I criticised your idea.' (Rola said this)
Rola ________.
choices: accused me of criticising my idea.

question:
'I will help you.' (He said this)
He agreed ________.
In reported questions, what is the formula for a YES/NO question?
In reported questions, what is the formula for a WH-question?
For affirmative commands in reported speech, the formula is:
For negative commands in reported speech, the formula is:
For affirmative requests (with 'Can/Will/Please'), the formula is:

'I didn't take a photo of her!' (Rana said this)
Rana ________.
choices: admitted taking a photo of her.
insisted on taking a photo of her.
denied taking a photo of her.
apologised for taking a photo of her.

question:
'I'll always remember you!' (Hamed said this)
Hamed promised that ________.
The teachers told us to read about the topic. 'Read about the topic.' The original sentence was a/an:
Noura's opinion on hunting is that hunting for sport is cruel, but perhaps it's OK if you eat the animal. Whose opinion on hunting is this?
ItsNoura added a question to the message board: 'Is there any behaviour you feel is unacceptable today?' She gave the example of:
choices: playing loud music on public transport

question: Ranagirl's answer to the message board focused on the topic of:
SamarS's answer to the message board was about:
choices: the need to protect animals and the cruelty of hunting for sport
reducing plastic waste in public areas

question: The correct pronunciation of the underlined letter in the word 'poor' is:
In word stress pairs, 'justify' and 'justifiable' have ________ word stress.
In word stress pairs, 'enforcement' and 'enforceable' have ________ word stress.

Read this passage and answer the question:

I've been trying to persuade people in my community to help an environmental charity by donating just a few coins each week. At first people were not keen, but my persistence has paid off and now a lot of people give me a little money – and that is really helping the charity.

Some people say you need a lot of money to help a charity. What does the author of this passage believe instead?
choices: That you need a very large amount of money to help charities
That determination and a little time are what you really need
That only young people should donate to charities
That social media is the best way to help charities

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

As far as I'm concerned, social media is the obvious way to get your message across. All you need to do is to pay for a good Internet connection which most people have anyway these days. On a more personal level, I let my friends know my views on social networks and I would support a campaign to protect animal rights.

According to this passage, what is needed to use social media effectively?
choices: A very expensive computer and fast internet
A good Internet connection which most people already have
Special training in social media marketing
A large number of followers on social networks

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

Last year, I joined a group that wanted to clean up one of the beaches near Aqaba, where I live. It is very popular with tourists who often leave litter there. I used to go to the beach every weekend and pick up the litter, all for free! I soon realised that volunteering like this helped but didn't solve the problem. The only way is to change tourists' attitudes to litter.

What does the author of this passage believe is the only real solution to the litter problem?
choices: Banning tourists from visiting the beach
Charging tourists a fee for visiting
